web.title: Wireless Carplay Retrofit for 2011-2016 Porsches with PCM 3.1
web.description: It should fit most Porsches from 2011-2016 with the factory navigation, but specifically these cars are confirmed to fit: 2012-2016 Porsche 911 (991.1) models ...
web.markdown:
![](https://i0.wp.com/www.nickscarblog.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/IMG_4342.jpeg?fit=1280%2C960&ssl=1)

[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https://www.nickscarblog.com/diy/wireless-carplay-retrofit-for-2011-2016-porsches-with-pcm-3-1 "Facebook") [Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=Wireless%20Carplay%20Retrofit%20for%202011-2016%20Porsches%20with%20PCM%203.1&url=https://www.nickscarblog.com/diy/wireless-carplay-retrofit-for-2011-2016-porsches-with-pcm-3-1&via=nickscarblog "Twitter") [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.nickscarblog.com/diy/wireless-carplay-retrofit-for-2011-2016-porsches-with-pcm-3-1&title=Wireless%20Carplay%20Retrofit%20for%202011-2016%20Porsches%20with%20PCM%203.1 "LinkedIn") [Reddit](https://reddit.com/submit?url=https://www.nickscarblog.com/diy/wireless-carplay-retrofit-for-2011-2016-porsches-with-pcm-3-1&title=Wireless%20Carplay%20Retrofit%20for%202011-2016%20Porsches%20with%20PCM%203.1 "Reddit") [Share via Email](mailto:?subject=Wireless%20Carplay%20Retrofit%20for%202011-2016%20Porsches%20with%20PCM%203.1&body=https://www.nickscarblog.com/diy/wireless-carplay-retrofit-for-2011-2016-porsches-with-pcm-3-1 "Share via Email")

In this post I’ll detail exactly how I retrofitted Wireless (and wired) Apply CarPlay to my 2014 Porsche Cayenne. This same product and DIY installation guide will apply to any other Porsches from 2011-2016 that has Porsche’s PCM 3.1 navigation unit including the 911, Panamera, Cayman, Macan, and Boxster.

I love my Cayenne – at nearly 100K miles it’s been a great daily driver for me and my family. I have had no real desire to get a newer model other than that the navigation/radio has become extremely dated over the years.

After seeing tons of posts on Facebook and Rennlist about people modifying their stock headunits to run Apple CarPlay and Android Auto, I finally bit the bullet and bought one of the retrofit kits myself.

I’ve seen them listed for as much as $600, but [the ISUDAR kit for approximately $300](https://amzn.to/3QbWAec) had great reviews and was cheap enough that if the installation proved too daunting, I could walk away with minimal downside…and you can pick it up on Amazon making it a little less sketchy than some of the online stores I came across in my research but wasn’t sure if they were legit or not.

Installation looks incredibly scary – you basically have to take your head-unit out, split it open, and install a new circuit board…but on a scale of 1-10, with 10 being the hardest DIY project I’ve ever done, I’d call this a 3 or 4. It looks way scarier than it is, it’s just time consuming and requires focus & concentration.

## Installation Process

YouTube

This DIY is for the ISUDAR board in particular, although I’d imagine the other options out there are very similar – essentially you add an additional board that piggy-backs or rides on top of the existing board, and this new motherboard is what powers your CarPlay and hijacks control from the normal PCM interface.

The kit comes with a step-by-step guide as well as a link to their official YouTube video, but I thought it’d be helpful the experience of an actual person installing it versus the manufacturer.

![](https://i0.wp.com/www.nickscarblog.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/IMG_2966-1.jpeg?resize=708%2C531&ssl=1)

The key thing is to take your time – each connection needs to be snug. Otherwise, it’s pretty straightforward as most connections can only go one way. The trickiest part is figuring out how to remove your radio, as Porsches don’t use standard radio keys like a lot of other cars but are instead bolted into the dashboard.

For the entire process, block out 2-3 hours for this, although once you’ve done one the next one should only take about half of that. If I were to do a third kit, I bet I could do it under an hour.

For more information, watch the video and I’ll walk you through the step-by-step!

## What It Fits In

It should fit most Porsches from 2011-2016 with the factory navigation, but specifically these cars are confirmed to fit:

- 2012-2016 Porsche 911 (991.1) models with PCM3.1
- 2012-2016 Porsche Boxster (981) models with PCM3.1
- 2012-2016 Porsche Cayman (981) models with PCM3.1
- 2011-2016 Porsche Cayenne (958.1) models with PCM3.1
- 2010-2016 Porsche Panamera (970) models with PCM 3.1
- 2014-2016 Porsche Macan (95B.1) models with PCM 3.1

If your radio looks like mine, then you likely have the PCM 3.1 and this is a direct fit.

## 90 Day Review

![](https://i0.wp.com/www.nickscarblog.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/IMG_3099.jpeg?resize=708%2C531&ssl=1)

I’ve now had this installed for about three months now, and I’m happy to report it works exactly as described. The only issue of note is that it occasionally can’t find my phone over Bluetooth, usually when I’ve been away for a while (e.g. not connected to the car in over a week) or if my wife and I are both in the car at the same time and it connects to the wrong phone (e.g. hers) first. In those cases you can either plug the car in the optional wired USB, or you can go into the settings and have it reconnect to your phone of choice…not a big deal, but obviously something you don’t want to do while driving.

Otherwise, it’s been an absolute game changer for the car…I love being able to listen to my music and podcasts without effort…before the upgrade, I had to manually select BT Audio each time, but since upgrading my phone automatically is connected via AUX IN and I don’t have to manually change the Source. This alone is worth the $300, yet alone all of the other features of CarPlay.

The other big feature is Google Maps or Waze at your fingertips, versus having to constantly look down at your phone. You can enter the address on your phone and it automatically appears in your dash, making navigation so much easier and better than either using your phone or the PCM.

Everything about the board seemed sturdy, and I have no doubt it will continue to work well but I’ll update this post if/when I run into any more issues.

## Where to Buy

![](https://i0.wp.com/www.nickscarblog.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/07/IMG_3363.png?resize=708%2C425&ssl=1)

You can buy this just about anywhere, but I recommend [Amazon](https://amzn.to/4557bMp) where you get free one-day shipping and know that Amazon will back you up if there is an issue with the seller. I’ve seen this kit sold elsewhere under different names for anywhere from $400-600, so this definitely seems to be the cheapest conversion kit I’ve found without sacrificing quality.

**Affiliate Disclosure:** The links in this post are affiliate links and I do make a commission from any purchases, and your patronage is appreciated if you found this review helpful. Ultimately I have this in my own car as well as installed in one of my best friend’s car, so this is a product I personally use and believe in.

### 15 Comments

01. Sweet write up. I personally went with the BimmerDirect.com module. The Amazon seller you linked barely spoke any English at all. Can’t really trust that. It is a good quality of life mod for sure.

02. Cool – how do you like that module so far? FWIW I’ve installed two of these and haven’t had any problem in 6 months. My suspicion is some websites are reselling the same chip/board under different names and their only real differentiator is marketing/ability to speak English…could be wrong though!

03. Nice review Nick and I just wanted to respond to your comment about these boards essentially being mostly the same and only differentiated by the sellers description etc. I had a similar suspicion so spent a bit of time closely examining install videos on youtube and a variety of Ads from car modifiers, auto electricians, Amazon, Ebay, etc. I came to the conclusion that there are three real variants. Most significantly the latest models dont require a separate box mounted behind the dash. The most recent also have better integration of reversing camera, parking sensors, etc. I have seen these on sale all the way up to £1K installed, so I ordered one from Ali Express for £156 delivered. I then installed myself exactly as you have and it does everything the most expensive alternatives offer.

04. Nick, as usual, a wonderful post with great insight. I’ve learned a lot from your blogs. Thank you for keeping us updated on the Cayenne. Gary, could you please provide the link for the seller at AliExpress where you purchased and recommend the latest version? I have a 2012 Cayenne S and I want to add Apple CarPlay.

05. Hello Nick, I purchased the kit from Autoabc and installed it. It is version 2 which allows you to use the Porsche’s original Mic. Can you advise the settings (within carplay) and some instructions so that while say I am using google maps or listening to the music from my iphone (AUX in) and the phone rings, I should be able to answer the phone by simply pressing the lift phone button and talk using my original Porsche microphones.

06. Question. Do you have Xmas now that you removed the board?

07. Thanks Nick, have you had loss of any PCM functionality? – such as tone control in the sound menu, the car/trip menus, and so on? I need to dig in further to better understand the basics of operation of the upgraded system overall

08. Everything else still works as before – the CarPlay is basically just an additional menu / screen when you long hold the Info button, but everything else is still there and functional.

09. I think you mean XM radio, and no I don’t.

10. Hi Gary, can you share the one you ordered and installed please ? Thanks

11. Can you give an update on how this is working out? I’m looking at purchasing a used Panamera and would definitely want to upgrade to support car play/PCM integration.

12. Hey Nick, I went to install it in a 2016 Boxter Spyder (you mentioned you did it on a friends car), but the second harness of wires (not the big square one) that comes off the motherboard has a bunch of connections that are not on the car side.

13. Great DIY guide, very clear steps.

14. Nick, I’m going to play the idiot, I have 50 years tech experience and worked on most makes and models. But on my Cayenne, I’m not just pulling the A/C ducts loose! I didn’t see that in your video. My radio died and I took to the dealer who wanted more than $5K, I took it to a well known audio shop and they screwed up and then sold out. I’ve done radios before but those A/C vents worry me. Plus did I see you needed to do a couple solder connections? Major Weed at k,weed2011@gmail.com

15. No soldering required! For the vents, you simply push in the inside. Check out this vid: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4vwi4hbKeo4](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4vwi4hbKeo4)

# About this item

- 【Applicable Models】:Wireless Carplay & Android auto Keep original screen, suit for PCM 3.1 system Porsche Panamera, Cayenne, Macan, Cayman, Boxster, 911, 718. Please confirm the car system, it only suits PCM 3.1. Supports Non-destructive installation, you don't need to change the original cable. Easy to install, better effect. Just fit PCM 3.1 System，not fit CDR , not fit PCM 3.0，not fit PCM 4.0 System.
- 【Powerful Processor】: SUNPLUS SPHE8368U, Cortex A9. It has more powerful processing power, Anti-interference, and High Stability. The Built-in 5GHz WiFi module has a more stable connection.
- 【Non-destructive installation】: 1. Supports steering wheel and radio buttons control. 2. Support original camera or aftermarket camera and parking sensor. 3. Support voice wakes up Siri & Google Assistant. 4. Porsche PCM 3.1 Carplay no need to use an external microphone. 5. No need to insert an extra box under the dashboard.
- 【Support Wired & Wireless Apple Carplay & Android Auto】:The mobile app will be displayed on the radio screen via the android mobiles connected to the car radio, Supports online maps, online music, messages...The wireless control only supports the mobile which has wireless Android Auto. Enjoy safe and smart driving.
- 【Support Android&IOS mirror link】: Mobile screens are displayed on the radio, the sound will be played by car speakers. Support U-Disk input, music, and video playback. Porsche PCM 3.1 carplay supports front and rearview camera input. Support DSP sound processor and EQ adjustment, You can adjust the sound style as you like. Support OEM Camera and Parking Sensor. Support CVBS/AHD camera( Aftermarket camera).

## Product description

![Andriod auto for pcm 3.1](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/52435341-41dc-4d8b-bc3d-9a8642767652.__CR0,0,970,600_PT0_SX970_V1___.jpg)

### When making a call, the navigation information is split-screen displayed. Having safer & smarter driving.

![Voice control for porsche](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/203b3d8c-09bd-4cb8-9d81-a49db6bfa74b.__CR0,0,300,400_PT0_SX300_V1___.jpg)

![Isudar](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/88ccf0b9-6e45-474c-b689-7cd9ed335b75.__CR0,0,350,175_PT0_SX350_V1___.jpg)

Isudar is a company specializing in the research and development, manufacturing and sales of Car Radio and Car peripheral products. Isudar has a strong independent R&D team and excellent manufacturing capabilities. With advanced technology, stable quality , Isudar has become a mainstream brand in the global car navigation industry.

#### Support Voice Control

After installing the Isudar Porsche PCM 3.1 wireless Carplay module, you can wake up Siri or Google Voice Control, you can control navigation, control music, adjust volume, make calls, text messages, etc., no need to operate the buttons by hands. Making your driving safer and smater.

- Support steering wheel key adjustment.
- Compatible with central control knob key operation.
- Support touch screen operation of OEM car radio.
- Does not affect the functions of the original vehicle.
- Support multimedia, music videos, and picture play via a USB port.

### This Carplay Module just support Porsche PCM 3.1 System. Please confirm the system of the car before purchase.

![porsche macan carplay](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/a8a2b68b-7035-4436-8d3f-ff3f00aeed5f.__CR0,0,970,600_PT0_SX970_V1___.jpg)

![Navigation for porsche](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/06ec88ec-78e7-47c4-9bb8-89d960e83acc.__CR0,0,300,300_PT0_SX300_V1___.jpg)

#### Smart Navigation

Support mainstream maps such as Google Maps, Waze, synchronize mobile phone data, search history, mobile phone online/offline maps, Make driving more convenient.

![Music multi media play for porsche](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/a670fa84-eff6-4c1a-a93e-eb1a3d9ac15a.__CR0,0,300,300_PT0_SX300_V1___.jpg)

#### Music Playback

Support mainstream music playback software, synchronize mobile phone data, non-destructive installation, and continue the sound quality of the original car.

![Mirro link for porsche panamera macan ](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/3f5f69e7-dd24-4792-9e76-c01e1f4764da.__CR0,0,300,300_PT0_SX300_V1___.jpg)

#### Mirror Link

Porsche wireless Carplay supports mirror link, You can watch videos online on car stereo via mirror link. (eg YouTube...). At same time, it supports USB stick, multimedia music video, and picture play via USB port.

![QA1](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/16452463-098d-4f6a-b34f-3f3f9cfe0924.__CR0,0,135,135_PT0_SX135_V1___.jpg)

### No sound, all connections are OK.

- Come into the OEM settings menu, find the audio settings, there are AUX and AUX Bluetooth, select them both. When playing sound through Carplay, select AUX channel on the OEM interface.
- If there is no AUX optional: Place a CD in the CD player and let it play, select CD as your source and play as normal. Press the Options button and a new hidden window will appear. Press the check box that says AUX to activate the port.
- Even if AUX is chosen, there is no sound: if the DISC can be read, the optical fiber of the original car is not plugged in properly; if the DISC cannot be read, it may be that the DVD Driver is not installed properly.

![QA2](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/21d03428-19c0-4b37-be3e-6a44ca5f373e.__CR0,0,135,135_PT0_SX135_V1___.jpg)

### Why the screen is black when long press "INFO" button?

- First, check whether the OEM radio works normally. Then, Check if the FPC cables are firmly inserted and fastened, whether they are damaged or installed backwards.

![QA3](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/2f1aa986-0a01-47a3-aba6-8d402b8f69e5.__CR0,0,135,135_PT0_SX135_V1___.jpg)

### Why is the connection signal of mobile phone poor and easy to disconnect？

- The antenna may be not installed properly or damaged. This can be replaced for free.

![QA4](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/858dbeb8-de0b-428f-914b-a43fc629a5b1.__CR0,0,135,135_PT0_SX135_V1___.jpg)

### Why the wireless Android Auto cannot be connected?

- Confirm whether the mobile phone supports a wireless connection. Except for Samsung and Google mobile phones, most other Andriod mobile phones support wireless connection from the Android 11 version.

![Easy install for porsche Boxster](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/b28d674b-ee42-4a97-a060-e4582c5a8053.__CR0,0,970,600_PT0_SX970_V1___.jpg)

- For more detailed installation instructions, please refer to the manual.
- We insist on providing high-quality products. Thank you for your trust and support of ISUDAR!

![Easy install](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/079b0843-4440-4d5e-b88a-f8470dfade06.__CR0,0,970,600_PT0_SX970_V1___.jpg)

## From the brand

![Isudar](https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/aplus-media-library-service-media/eea526cb-e240-4f1a-b040-ca38cb0d497b.__CR0,0,1464,625_PT0_SX1464_V1___.png)

Isudar is a brand focusing on car audio, navigator, driving recorders and other in-vehicle electronic products. Our brand story began in 2009. The founder was an expert in car audio technology. He knew the importance of car audio to the driving experience, so he founded the Isudar brand, aiming to provide car owners with more high-quality car electronic products and Serve.

#### Does this work for 2011 Porsche Cayenne? Is any stock functionality lost at all?



This will not affect the functions of the original car.



You need to confirm that your vehicle's system is PCM 3.1 system. Usually, the system with a "NAVI" button in the central control is PCM 3.1 system, which is compatible.

I am so pleased with the ISUDAR Apple CarPlay kit I installed in my 2014 Porsche Cayenne (PCM 3.1). It comes on almost immediately after starting the car and performs flawlessly. And all of the OEM equipment capabilities are still easily accessible if I ever need them.







I did experience some challenges/ learnings installing it so highly recommend watching all the videos and carefully reading through the installation instructions. Along the way I managed to damage one of the ribbon cables and one of the data cables. Thankfully, after reaching out to ISUDAR tech support, they promptly sent out replacements for both.







Some key learnings & tips to pass along:



1) Pay attention to the direction of the ribbon cables (blue band facing direction) if you need to detach both ends of a ribbon cable. It matters.



2) Heed the instructions provided on proper ribbon cable seating



3) Don’t detach the clips holding the ribbon cables, ever so gently pull them upwards until the cable releases. The clips can easily break.



4) If you find yourself detaching the front display from its ribbon cables make sure when you reassemble you attach the ribbon cables to the display first and then the other end to the ISUDAR sister board. This will provide you ample hand room to achieve a proper seating. Note this, if you reassemble the unit and you get a black screen a poor ribbon cable seating is likely your issue.



5) Inspect the wires coming into your OEM power connector to see if your vehicle is equipped with fiber optic sound system. If it is you will need to relocate those two fiber optic cables from the OEM power connector to the ISUDAR power connector. If your vehicle has fiber optic sound, they will be the two cables in the lower left-hand side as you look at the rear of the OEM connector. No sound and an inability to switch to other sources, e.g. FM, AM, SAT, AUX, should be your first clue that this is the issue.

I used this product to add Apple CarPlay functionality to the PCM in my Porsche. Installing the ISUDAR board was straightforward and didn’t require any specialized knowledge. However, I highly recommend reading the installation guide thoroughly and watching the suggested video tutorial before getting started. Patience is key—don’t rush the process!







Summary of Installation Steps:



1\. Carefully removed the PCM unit from the car and detached the CD unit.



2\. Disconnected three ribbon cables from the original OEM board.



3\. Installed three pins to create a platform for the new ISUDAR board.



4\. Routed the ribbon cables through the ISUDAR board and connected the supplied antenna.







Pro Tip: Take photos of all connectors and cables \*before unplugging them. This will make reassembly much easier, especially with flat cables, which can be tricky to position correctly.







While the product works well, the included paper manual could use significant improvement. For example, the instructions on reconnecting the fiber optic cables (essential for Bose systems) were easy to overlook. Additionally, the first-time setup could be clearer—though in my case, resetting the PCM allowed it to recognize the ISUDAR board quickly, and it smoothly guided me through the CarPlay setup process.







Note to the Manufacturer: Improving the clarity of the instructions wouldn’t require much effort. Adding a troubleshooting section to the manual would also be a welcome enhancement.

I used this product to add Apple CarPlay functionality to the PCM in my Porsche. Installing the ISUDAR board was straightforward and didn’t require any specialized knowledge. However, I highly recommend reading the installation guide thoroughly and watching the suggested video tutorial before getting started. Patience is key—don’t rush the process!







Summary of Installation Steps:



1\. Carefully removed the PCM unit from the car and detached the CD unit.



2\. Disconnected three ribbon cables from the original OEM board.



3\. Installed three pins to create a platform for the new ISUDAR board.



4\. Routed the ribbon cables through the ISUDAR board and connected the supplied antenna.







Pro Tip: Take photos of all connectors and cables \*before unplugging them. This will make reassembly much easier, especially with flat cables, which can be tricky to position correctly.







While the product works well, the included paper manual could use significant improvement. For example, the instructions on reconnecting the fiber optic cables (essential for Bose systems) were easy to overlook. Additionally, the first-time setup could be clearer—though in my case, resetting the PCM allowed it to recognize the ISUDAR board quickly, and it smoothly guided me through the CarPlay setup process.







Note to the Manufacturer: Improving the clarity of the instructions wouldn’t require much effort. Adding a troubleshooting section to the manual would also be a welcome enhancement.

I purchased this for my daughter’s 2013 Porsche Cayenne base so we could add CarPlay to this really nice vehicle. I installed following all the instructions and it worked for about a day. Then it started randomly switching the audio source from CarPlay to a random radio station. Some times this would happen a few times a day, sometimes it would happen every 5 minutes. This bricked the isudar device. Luckily it did not hurt the radio and after removing the isudar device I have the radio back to factory. This was an incredible waste of time and money. I suggest finding a device that has US based (or US time) support. Be aware, all of these devices seem to come with an extra wiring harness that you’ll have to try to fit into the small opening behind the radio, in addition to the factory wiring harness. This completely updated our Cayenne. I don’t know why I waited years to purchase this. It may have been due to installation involved. In reality, the installation was really easy. There are a few nuances to be aware of, such as using higher stand off’s to clear satellite radio (you can’t just remove that - it affects other things. Some DIY videos don’t acknowledge that). Also, with the additional wiring harness required, fitment gets really tight when reinstalling the head unit.







Aside from that, this thing is awesome. It works really well and does exactly what you would expect while maintaining the OEM look of the car (yes, backup camera still functions as normal). I do have to occasionally re-pair my phone or restart the unit when it acts up but, it isnt often.







This thing is really well engineered and just works well. It’s like getting a new car in a way given the horrendous UI the cayenne comes with for this generation 958.2). I also have a 911 with CarPlay installed from manufacturer and this thing is honestly better considering it has wireless connectivity.

##### Great Solution for CarPlay – With a Few Installation Quirks



Reviewed in the United Kingdom on July 3, 2025




[Verified Purchase](https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html/ref=cm_cr_dp_d_rvw_avp?ie=UTF8&nodeId=G8UYX7LALQC8V9KA)








Brief content visible, double tap to read full content.



Full content visible, double tap to read brief content.

















I’ve only had this CarPlay module installed for a couple of days, but so far, I’m really impressed. It arrived the next day, which was a big plus. I was originally considering a replacement head unit from AliExpress, but the fact that I could keep my original Porsche system and simply add this module made the decision easy.







While the product was listed as compatible with my 2015 Macan, there were a few small discrepancies I wish I had known in advance. For example, I had to double up on some pins during installation, and the green power cable didn’t fit where the instructions suggested — I had to find a workaround. Not a huge issue if you’re handy (I’m comfortable building computers), but worth noting.







The installation process took about 3 hours and had its stressful moments. One frustrating bit was reattaching the CD cover — the added bulk from the new cables made it a tight fit. Better instructions or clearer video guides would have really helped here.







Functionally, it works well and retains most of the car’s original features, which was my main goal. However, there are a few caveats:



• The front parking sensors don’t show on screen unless the car is in reverse — they only beep otherwise.



• The CarPlay connects via WiFi, which interfered with my dash cam’s connection.



• Initially, I had the module on 5G WiFi, but the screen froze and even went fuzzy once. Restarting the engine fixed it, and switching to 2G seems to have solved the issue.



• The screen brightness feels a bit low, and there doesn’t seem to be an option to adjust it.







Despite these minor issues, I’m really happy with the result. The audio quality remains strong, the touchscreen is responsive, and overall, this is a much better solution than replacing the whole unit. For anyone moderately tech-savvy, I’d definitely recommend it — just be prepared for a bit of a learning curve during installation.
